title: Eranthemum pulchellum
taxonomy: Eranthemum pulchellum is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It is native to tropical Asia and is a popular ornamental plant due to its attractive flowers and compact growth habit. The genus Eranthemum belongs to the tribe Ocimeae within the Lamiaceae family. This classification suggests a close relationship with other plants in the same tribe, such as Plectranthus and Coleus. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Eranthemum pulchellum.

intro: Eranthemum pulchellum, commonly known as the blue porterweed, is a flowering plant species in the family Acanthaceae.
